notes: Gold consumable rules for ai_exposure table. 389 rows, grain = soc_code. All 7 spec-mandated rules written. Inverse invariant holds universally (no exposure_score=0 rows in Gold subset). No golden dataset exists yet -- CONS-GOLDEN-DATASET rule DEFERRED pending golden dataset creation by @primary-agent. Concept normalization patterns (CONS-COLLISION-RESOLVED, CONS-COVERAGE-FLOOR) do not apply -- single-source spec with no concept normalization at Gold level (broad expansion handled at Silver).

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.ai_exposure GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per soc_code. Any duplicate indicates a promote or transformer failure. Maps to patterns CONS-GRAIN-UNIQUE and ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '389 distinct soc_code values across 389 rows. Zero duplicates.' Physical model: grain = soc_code, UNIQUE constraint. Threshold: 100% unique.

rules.name: Record ID uniqueness and not null
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.ai_exposure GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'aie') must be non-null and unique. Duplicate or null record_id indicates a grain hash collision or derivation bug.
rules.rationale: Physical model: 'record_id is PK, NOT NULL.' Deterministic SHA-256 hash of natural key (soc_code) should produce 0 collisions for 389 rows. Maps to ADV-GRAIN-UNIQUE.

rules.name: Row count within expected range (370-409)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) BETWEEN 370 AND 409 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.ai_exposure
rules.description: Gold table must contain 389 +/- 5% rows (370-409). Row count is determined by Silver rows where bls_match=true. Maps to ADV-ENTITY-COVERAGE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '389 rows after bls_match=true filter.' Spec: 'Row count: matches Silver filtered count.' EDA recommends 389 +/- 5% (370-409) to accommodate future source changes. Threshold: count between 370 and 409.

rules.name: stat_res range: 1-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE stat_res IS NULL OR stat_res < 1 OR stat_res > 10
rules.description: stat_res (AI Resilience) must be an integer in [1, 10]. Derived as MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10). Values outside this range indicate a derivation bug. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE and ADV-VALUE-RANGE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Range: 1-10 (full range exercised).' Physical model CHECK constraint: stat_res >= 1 AND stat_res <= 10. 0 violations in 389 rows. Threshold: 100% valid.

rules.name: boss_ai_score range: 1-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE boss_ai_score IS NULL OR boss_ai_score < 1 OR boss_ai_score > 10
rules.description: boss_ai_score (Fight AI boss strength) must be an integer in [1, 10]. Derived as MAX(exposure_score, 1). Values outside this range indicate a derivation bug. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE and ADV-VALUE-RANGE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Range: 1-10 (full range exercised).' Physical model CHECK constraint: boss_ai_score >= 1 AND boss_ai_score <= 10. 0 violations in 389 rows. Threshold: 100% valid.

rules.name: exposure_score range: 0-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score IS NULL OR exposure_score < 0 OR exposure_score > 10
rules.description: exposure_score (original Karpathy score) must be an integer in [0, 10]. Passthrough from Silver. Physical model CHECK constraint. Maps to CONS-IMPOSSIBLE-VALUE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Range: 1-10' (actual data). Physical model allows 0-10 per rubric definition. Domain context: 'Score 0 absent -- actual range is 1-10, not 0-10 as rubric allows.' DQ rule uses 0-10 defensively per domain context guidance. 0 violations in 389 rows.

rules.name: Inverse invariant: stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score >= 1 AND (stat_res + boss_ai_score) != 11
rules.description: For all rows where exposure_score >= 1, stat_res + boss_ai_score must equal 11. This invariant is guaranteed by the derivation math: (11 - x) + x = 11. Any violation indicates a derivation bug. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 for all 389 rows (100.0%). No violations.' All 389 Gold rows have exposure_score >= 1. The invariant holds universally. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Rationale non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE rationale IS NULL OR LENGTH(TRIM(rationale)) = 0
rules.description: rationale is a required display field for the Fight AI boss narrative in the FutureProof frontend. Must be non-null and non-empty.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Rationale is 100% complete. Zero nulls. Minimum length 297 characters.' Physical model: NOT NULL. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Rationale minimum length >= 100 characters
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE LENGTH(rationale) < 100
rules.description: Rationale text should be substantive (at least 100 characters). Short rationales would indicate truncation or corruption. Maps to ADV-VALUE-RANGE.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Minimum length 297 characters, maximum 587, median 404.' Silver DQ rule uses 100 char minimum. All 389 rows exceed this floor by a wide margin. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Cross-validation: soc_code exists in occupation_profiles
rules.sql: SELECT ae.soc_code FROM consumable.ai_exposure ae LEFT JOIN consumable.occupation_profiles op ON ae.soc_code = op.soc_code WHERE op.soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: Every soc_code in ai_exposure must exist in consumable.occupation_profiles. These tables must be joinable for downstream backfill into program_career_paths and career_branches. Maps to ADV-FK-VALID.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '100% cross-validation pass. All 389 SOC codes exist in consumable.occupation_profiles (832 rows).' Spec: 'Cross-validation with occupation_profiles: every soc_code in ai_exposure must exist in consumable.occupation_profiles (P0).' Threshold: 0 orphan SOC codes.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR soc_code !~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'
rules.description: Every soc_code must match the SOC standard format XX-XXXX. This is the primary join key for occupation_profiles and downstream tables.
rules.rationale: EDA report: 'Pattern: XX-XXXX format, all validated.' Physical model CHECK constraint: soc_code ~ '^\d{2}-\d{4}$'. 0 violations in 389 rows. Threshold: 100% valid.

rules.name: All required fields non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE record_id IS NULL OR soc_code IS NULL OR occupation_title IS NULL OR exposure_score IS NULL OR stat_res IS NULL OR boss_ai_score IS NULL OR rationale IS NULL OR category IS NULL OR promoted_at IS NULL
rules.description: All 9 columns in consumable.ai_exposure are NOT NULL per the physical model. Any null value indicates a transformer or promote failure.
rules.rationale: Physical model: all columns are NOT NULL. EDA report: 0% null rate across all profiled fields. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: stat_res derivation consistency: MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE stat_res != LEAST(11 - exposure_score, 10)
rules.description: stat_res must equal MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10) for every row. Validates the derivation formula was applied correctly, not just the output range. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: Physical model derivation: 'stat_res = min(11 - exposure_score, 10)'. EDA confirms the formula holds for all 389 rows. This is a stronger check than GLD-AIE-004 (range only) -- it validates the actual computation.

rules.name: boss_ai_score derivation consistency: MAX(exposure_score, 1)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE boss_ai_score != GREATEST(exposure_score, 1)
rules.description: boss_ai_score must equal MAX(exposure_score, 1) for every row. Validates the derivation formula was applied correctly, not just the output range. Maps to ADV-CROSS-COLUMN.
rules.rationale: Physical model derivation: 'boss_ai_score = max(exposure_score, 1)'. EDA confirms the formula holds for all 389 rows. This is a stronger check than GLD-AIE-005 (range only) -- it validates the actual computation.

rules.name: Coverage: ai_exposure SOC codes as percentage of occupation_profiles
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT ae.soc_code) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(CAST((SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT soc_code) FROM consumable.occupation_profiles) AS DOUBLE), 0) < 0.40 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM consumable.ai_exposure ae
rules.description: ai_exposure should cover at least 40% of occupation_profiles SOC codes. Current coverage is 46.8% (389/832). A drop below 40% would indicate significant data loss. Tracked for monitoring.
rules.rationale: EDA report: '389 of 832 occupation_profiles SOCs have AI exposure data (46.8%).' Coverage is expected to be partial -- Karpathy scored 342 OOH occupations, broad expansion brings it to 389. Threshold: >= 40% (floor well below current 46.8% to allow for minor Silver changes).

rules.name: Blended row count within union band (820-860)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(*) BETWEEN 820 AND 860 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation FROM consumable.ai_exposure
rules.description: Post-v4 blended table should contain ~840 rows (798 Gemma O*NET coverage ∪ ~42 Karpathy-only SOCs). Band accepts modest drift in the Karpathy-only tail.
rules.rationale: v4 spec §4 — coverage expectation 798 Gemma + ~42 Karpathy-only. Before S1 is run this rule fires; that's expected until Gemma batch is complete. After v4 is shipped the band tightens.

rules.name: scoring_model ∈ {'gemma-4', 'gemini-flash'}
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE scoring_model IS NULL OR scoring_model NOT IN ('gemma-4', 'gemini-flash')
rules.description: scoring_model must be one of the two supported provenance tokens. Anything else indicates a blender bug.
rules.rationale: v4 §4 blending truth table — only two allowed values.

rules.name: No Unknown category
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE category IS NULL OR category = 'Unknown'
rules.description: Every row must have a category — either inherited from Karpathy or derived from SOC 2018 major group. 'Unknown' indicates derive_category fell through and hollows the A/B category-bias gate.
rules.rationale: v4 §4 derive_category — SOC major group mapping is 100% coverage for valid BLS SOCs.

rules.name: model_tag present for every Gemma-scored row
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE scoring_model = 'gemma-4' AND model_tag IS NULL
rules.description: Row-level reproducibility: every Gemma-scored row must carry the Ollama model tag so auditors can trace scores to a specific model version.
rules.rationale: v4 §4 — model_tag stamped per row by scorer, preserved through Bronze/Silver/Gold. Karpathy rows legitimately have model_tag = NULL.

rules.name: composite_exposure within 0-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE composite_exposure IS NOT NULL AND (composite_exposure < 0 OR composite_exposure > 10)
rules.description: S4 v4 — composite_exposure must live on the 0-10 scale. Out-of-range values would cascade to stat_res and boss_ai_score.
rules.rationale: spec §4 — composite is clamped to 0-10 in compute_composite via max(0, min(10, composite)).

rules.name: stat_res and boss_ai_score consistent with composite_exposure
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE composite_exposure IS NOT NULL AND (stat_res <> LEAST(11 - composite_exposure, 10) OR boss_ai_score <> GREATEST(composite_exposure, 1))
rules.description: stat_res = MIN(11 - composite_exposure, 10) and boss_ai_score = MAX(composite_exposure, 1). Divergence indicates the transformer forgot to re-derive after computing composite.
rules.rationale: spec §4 derive_stats — guarantees downstream pentagon/boss displays agree with the composite.

rules.name: adoption_percentile within 0-100
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE adoption_percentile IS NOT NULL AND (adoption_percentile < 0 OR adoption_percentile > 100)
rules.description: adoption_percentile is a percent_rank * 100 output and must live in [0, 100].
rules.rationale: spec §4 percent_rank — monotonic rank transform bounded to [0, 100] by construction.

rules.name: confidence_weight within 0.3-1.0
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE confidence_weight IS NOT NULL AND (confidence_weight < 0.3 OR confidence_weight > 1.0)
rules.description: confidence_weight must live in [0.3, 1.0] (or 0.5 fallback). Out-of-range values indicate a clamp bug.
rules.rationale: spec §4 — compute_composite clamps to max(0.3, min(1.0, ...)). Fallback value 0.5 also within range.

rules.name: velocity_label belongs to enum
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE velocity_label IS NOT NULL AND velocity_label NOT IN ('saturating','accelerating','emerging','nascent','unknown')
rules.description: velocity_label must be one of: saturating / accelerating / emerging / nascent / unknown. Unrecognized values break the narrative prompt switch.
rules.rationale: spec §4 velocity_from_percentile — total function over the enum.

rules.name: composite_method belongs to enum
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM consumable.ai_exposure WHERE composite_method IS NOT NULL AND composite_method NOT IN ('three_signal','two_signal_no_anthropic','gemma_plus_anthropic','gemma_only','karpathy_only','observed_override','no_data')
rules.description: composite_method must be one of the 7 documented provenance values. Anything else indicates a fallthrough bug in compute_composite.
rules.rationale: spec §4 — every branch returns a labeled method.
